subtopic: 12-Factor Agents — principles for production-grade LLM apps

source.title: 12-Factor Agents — Principles for building reliable LLM applications
source.author: Dexter Horthy (HumanLayer)

framing: Inspired by 12factor.net. Argues that production-grade agents are 'mostly just software with LLM steps sprinkled in,' not the naive 'prompt + tools + loop' pattern. Targets engineers who got to 70-80% with a framework and need to go past that.

factors.name: Natural Language to Tool Calls
factors.summary: Convert user intent to structured tool calls — the irreducible LLM step.

factors.name: Own your prompts
factors.summary: Don't let a framework hide them; prompts are core IP and need version control.

factors.name: Own your context window
factors.summary: Context engineering — explicitly curate what goes into each LLM call.

factors.name: Tools are just structured outputs
factors.summary: A 'tool call' is just JSON the LLM emits; no magic — treat as structured output.

factors.name: Unify execution state and business state
factors.summary: Don't keep separate 'agent state' and 'app state' — collapse them.

factors.name: Launch/Pause/Resume with simple APIs
factors.summary: Agents must be pausable and resumable, like any long-running job.

factors.name: Contact humans with tool calls
factors.summary: Human-in-the-loop is just another tool the agent can call.

factors.name: Own your control flow
factors.summary: Don't outsource the main loop to a framework; write it yourself.

factors.name: Compact errors into context window
factors.summary: Surface and summarize errors back to the LLM so it can recover.

factors.name: Small, focused agents
factors.summary: Prefer many small agents over one large one — they're easier to reason about.

factors.name: Trigger from anywhere, meet users where they are
factors.summary: Agents should be invocable from Slack, email, cron, webhook — not just chat.

factors.name: Make your agent a stateless reducer
factors.summary: next_state = f(current_state, event) — pure-function semantics enable replay, testing, distribution.

honorable_mention.name: Pre-fetch all the context you might need
honorable_mention.summary: Front-load retrieval so the agent doesn't pay tool-call round-trips for known context.

key_insight: Most products billed as 'AI agents' are mostly deterministic code with LLM steps inserted at the right points — and the good ones intentionally avoid the naive 'prompt + tools + loop until done' pattern.
complements: llm-patterns-anthropic.json (Building Effective Agents) — they agree on simple > complex
llm-agent-orchestration-microsoft.json — 12-factor is single-agent engineering; MS is multi-agent coordination
